paragraph: The single-family market in Upper Saddle River is competitive but not frenzied. With 36 sales over six months at a median of $1,675,000, you're looking at steady volume in a town where homes range from $840,000 to $4,800,000 depending on location and lot size. About 42 percent of homes sold above asking, and the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.0, meaning sellers are getting their list price on average but not commanding premiums. Median days on market is 40, which tells you that priced homes are moving, though not instantaneously. The market rewards clear pricing and good condition, and buyers do have some ability to negotiate if a home sits. Condo and townhouse inventory is minimal at three sales in the same period, all landing near $1,200,000 at a median, with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.98. This tier moves slowly enough that you'll want to price strategically if you're selling, but it's too thin to read as a meaningful market segment. If you're considering Upper Saddle River, you're really evaluating single-family homes and their specific location and condition within town. Market heat is secondary to finding the right property at the right price.
